---
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
bibliography: refs.bib

---

```{r, Packages}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(lubridate)
library(FSA)
library(gt)
library(tinytable)
library(here)
library(english)
```

```{r, define spell_nr}

spell_nr <- function(x){
  if (x <= 9){
    as.character(english::english(x))
  } else{
    x
  } 
}
```

```{r, create full df}
#| include: false
# Get the boat data from the data cleaning script for debugging only
# boat <- readRDS("./temp/boat_data.rds")
# 
# boat <- boat %>% 
#   select(date.x, species, group_size, behaviour, geometry) %>% 
#   mutate(
#     source = "boat"
#   )
# 
# land <- data %>% 
#   select(date.x, species, group_size, behaviour, sighting_poi) %>% 
#   rename(geometry = sighting_poi) %>% 
#   mutate(
#     source = "land"
#   )
# 
# full <- boat %>%
#   bind_rows(land)
# 
# saveRDS(full, "./temp/full.rds")
```



```{r, Read data}
#| include: false
# Excel sheet of the spots with details
spots <- read_excel(here("data", "datasheet_landsurvey.xlsx"),sheet ="spots")


# Clean the sighting data and import
source("./land_survey.R")

full <- readRDS("./temp/full.rds")


# Set active spots as variable
active_spots <- spots %>%  
  filter(spot_name != "MedSteps" & spot_name != "WestSide") %>% 
  select(spot_name)

odontoceti <- c("common_dolphin", "striped_dolphin", "bottlenose_dolphin", "delphinidae")
mysteciti <- c("fin_whale")

```

```{r}
#|include: false

by_species <- data %>% 
  summarise(
    n_species = n(),
    .by = "species"
  ) %>% 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = species,
    values_from = n_species
  )

by_familiy <- full %>% 
  filter(source == "land") %>% 
  mutate(family = case_when(
    species %in% odontoceti ~ "odontoceti",
    species %in% mysteciti ~ "mysteciti",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>% 
  summarise(
    sighting = n(),
    .by = "family"
  ) %>% 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = family,
    values_from = sighting
  )


per_spot <- data %>% 
  summarise(
    sightings = n(),
    .by = "spot_name"
  )


full_source <- full %>% 
  summarise(
    sightings = n(),
    .by = "source"
  ) %>% 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = source,
    values_from = sightings
  )

full_sum <- full %>% 
  summarise(
    sightings = n()
  )

boat_species <- full %>% 
  filter(source == "boat") %>% 
  summarise(
    n_species = n(),
    .by = "species"
  ) %>% 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = species,
    values_from = n_species
  )

boat <- full %>% 
  filter(source == "boat")


```

```{r, Effort analysis}
#|include: false

effort <- effort_log %>% 
  mutate(date = dmy(date)) %>% 
  filter(date > "2026-05-28") %>% 
  mutate(across(c(observation_start, observation_end),
                ~ hms::as_hms(.))) %>% 
  filter(!grepl("off_effort", survey_id)) %>% 
  mutate(effort_time = as.duration(observation_end - observation_start))

er <- data %>%  
  filter(spot_name %in% active_spots$spot_name) %>% 
  summarise(min_group = min(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            max_group = max(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            mean_group = mean(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            median_group = median(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_group = sd(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            min_dis = min(distance, na.rm = TRUE),
            max_dis = max(distance, na.rm = TRUE),
            sightings = n(),
            .by = "spot_name") %>%
  left_join(effort %>%
              group_by(spot_name) %>% 
  summarise(hours = sum(effort_time)/60^2), by = "spot_name") %>% 
  mutate(per_hour = round(sightings/hours, 2)) %>% 
  mutate(group_range = paste(min_group, "-", max_group)) %>% 
  select(spot_name, sightings, hours, per_hour, min_dis, max_dis, group_range, mean_group, median_group, sd_group) %>% 
  arrange(desc(per_hour))


total_land <- effort %>% 
  summarise(
    surveys = n()
  )

species <- data %>%  
  filter(spot_name %in% active_spots$spot_name) %>% 
  summarise(max_group = max(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            min_group = min(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            max_dis = max(distance, na.rm = TRUE),
            min_dis = min(distance, na.rm = TRUE),
            sightings = n(),
            .by = "species")  %>% 
  arrange(desc(sightings))


```

```{r, Overview Table by Spot}
library(dplyr)
library(tinytable)

## --- helpers -----------------------------------------------------------------
## NA (not Inf) for all-missing subsets; en-dash range, em-dash for empty.
smin <- function(x) { x <- x[!is.na(x)]; if (length(x)) min(x) else NA_real_ }
smax <- function(x) { x <- x[!is.na(x)]; if (length(x)) max(x) else NA_real_ }
rng  <- function(a, b) ifelse(is.na(a) | is.na(b), "—", paste0(a, " – ", b))
## NA/NaN-safe numeric formatting (single-sighting species -> SD is NA)
fmt  <- function(x, d) ifelse(is.na(x), "—", sprintf(paste0("%.", d, "f"), x))

pretty_species <- function(x) {
  map <- c(common_dolphin = "Common Dolphin",
           delphinidae     = "Delphinidae",
           fin_whale       = "Fin Whale",
           unidentified    = "Unidentified")
  out  <- unname(map[x])
  miss <- is.na(out)
  out[miss] <- tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", x[miss]))
  out
}

active <- data %>% filter(spot_name %in% active_spots$spot_name)

## --- 1. spot-level summary (== your `er` table) ------------------------------
spot_sum <- active %>%
  summarise(
    sightings    = n(),
    d_min        = round(smin(distance)),
    d_max        = round(smax(distance)),
    g_min        = smin(group_size),
    g_max        = smax(group_size),
    g_mean       = mean(group_size,   na.rm = TRUE),
    g_median     = median(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
    g_sd         = sd(group_size,     na.rm = TRUE),
    .by = spot_name
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    effort %>% group_by(spot_name) %>%
      summarise(hours = sum(effort_time) / 60^2, .groups = "drop"),
    by = "spot_name"
  ) %>%
  mutate(per_hour = round(sightings / hours, 2),
         hours    = round(hours, 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(per_hour))            # spot ordering = descending encounter rate

## --- 2. species-level split within each spot ---------------------------------
sp_sum <- active %>%
  summarise(
    sightings = n(),
    d_min     = round(smin(distance)),
    d_max     = round(smax(distance)),
    g_min     = smin(group_size),
    g_max     = smax(group_size),
    g_mean    = mean(group_size,   na.rm = TRUE),
    g_median  = median(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
    g_sd      = sd(group_size,     na.rm = TRUE),
    .by = c(spot_name, species)
  ) %>%
  arrange(match(spot_name, spot_sum$spot_name), desc(sightings))

## --- 3. build interleaved display rows ---------------------------------------
spot_order <- tibble(spot_name = spot_sum$spot_name,
                     .ord = seq_along(spot_sum$spot_name))

spot_rows <- spot_sum %>%
  transmute(
    spot_name,
    label                = spot_name,
    Sightings            = as.character(sightings),
    `Distance range (m)` = rng(d_min, d_max),
    `Group range`        = rng(g_min, g_max),
    Hours                = sprintf("%.1f", hours),
    `Sightings·h⁻¹`      = sprintf("%.2f", per_hour),
    `Mean group`         = fmt(g_mean, 2),
    `Median group`       = fmt(g_median, 1),
    `SD group`           = fmt(g_sd, 2),
    .is_spot = TRUE, .sub = 0
  )

sp_rows <- sp_sum %>%
  transmute(
    spot_name,
    label                = paste0("\u2003", pretty_species(species)),   # indent species
    Sightings            = as.character(sightings),
    `Distance range (m)` = rng(d_min, d_max),
    `Group range`        = rng(g_min, g_max),
    Hours = "", `Sightings·h⁻¹` = "",
    `Mean group`         = fmt(g_mean, 2),
    `Median group`       = fmt(g_median, 1),
    `SD group`           = fmt(g_sd, 2),
    .is_spot = FALSE
  ) %>%
  group_by(spot_name) %>% mutate(.sub = row_number()) %>% ungroup()

combined <- bind_rows(spot_rows, sp_rows) %>%
  left_join(spot_order, by = "spot_name") %>%
  arrange(.ord, .sub)

spot_pos <- which(combined$.is_spot)          # row indices to emphasise

disp <- combined %>%
  select("Spot / Species" = label, Sightings, Hours,
         "Sightings per hour" = `Sightings·h⁻¹`,
         "Distance from observer" = `Distance range (m)`,
         "Range"  = `Group range`,
         "Mean"   = `Mean group`,
         "Median" = `Median group`,
         "SD"     = `SD group`)

## --- 4. render ---------------------------------------------------------------
## columns: 1 label | 2 Sightings | 3 Hours | 4 rate | 5 Distance | 6-9 Group block
overview_tbl <- tt(
  disp, width = c(4.7, 2, 1.3, 3, 4, 2.5, 2, 2, 2)
) %>%
  group_tt(j = list("Group Size" = 6:9)) %>%      # spanner over Range/Mean/Median/SD
  style_tt(j = 2:9, align = "c") %>%
  style_tt(i = spot_pos, bold = TRUE, background = "#212842", color = "white") %>%
  style_tt(i = -1:0, bold = TRUE)                 # bold spanner + header rows
#overview_tbl

## Optional export
# save_tt(overview_tbl, "summary_table.html", overwrite = TRUE)  # or .docx / .tex / .png
```


```{r}
#| include: false
#| output: false

effort <- effort_log %>%
  filter(!str_detect(survey_id, "off_effort")) %>%
  mutate(date = dmy(date)) %>% 
  filter(date > "2026-05-28") %>% 
  mutate(
    duration_min = as.numeric(difftime(observation_end, observation_start,
                                        units = "mins"))
  )
 
# ---- 3. Detections: keep on-effort sightings -------------------------------
sightings <- detection %>%
  filter(on_effort == 1)

 
# ---- 4. Sightings & individuals per scan -----------------------------------
per_scan <- sightings %>%
  group_by(survey_id) %>%
  summarise(
    n_sightings   = n(),
    n_individuals = sum(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),  # one NA group size -> 0
    .groups = "drop"
  )
 
# Join back onto the full effort table; scans with no sighting become 0 (NOT NA)
effort <- effort %>%
  left_join(per_scan, by = "survey_id") %>%
  mutate(
    n_sightings   = replace_na(n_sightings, 0),
    n_individuals = replace_na(n_individuals, 0)
  )
 
# ---- 5. SPUE by spot -------------------------------------------------------
spue_by_spot <- effort %>%
  group_by(spot_name) %>%
  summarise(
    scans        = n(),
    effort_hours = sum(duration_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    sightings    = sum(n_sightings),
    individuals  = sum(n_individuals),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    spue_per_scan   = sightings / scans,        # headline SPUE
    sightings_per_h = sightings / effort_hours, # encounter rate
    indiv_per_h     = individuals / effort_hours
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(scans))
 
# ---- 6. SPUE overall -------------------------------------------------------
spue_overall <- effort %>%
  summarise(
    spot_name    = "TOTAL",
    scans        = n(),
    effort_hours = sum(duration_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    sightings    = sum(n_sightings),
    individuals  = sum(n_individuals)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    spue_per_scan   = sightings / scans,
    sightings_per_h = sightings / effort_hours,
    indiv_per_h     = individuals / effort_hours
  )
 
spue_summary <- bind_rows(spue_by_spot, spue_overall)
 
# ---- 7. Report & export ----------------------------------------------------
spue_report <- spue_summary %>%
  mutate(across(c(effort_hours, spue_per_scan, sightings_per_h, indiv_per_h),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))
 
#print(spue_report, n = Inf)



```

```{r, Kruskal-Wallis and Dunn test}
 #|include: false

# Include the right df from datavis script

 active <- c("Europa", "SandyBay", "PrinceGeorge")
 scans  <- effort %>% filter(spot_name %in% active) # per-scan rows, NOT the summary

# count(scans, spot_name)


 SPUE_kru <- kruskal.test(n_sightings ~ spot_name, data = scans)

 SPUE_dunn <- dunnTest(n_sightings ~ factor(spot_name), data = scans, method = "bh")

```

```{r, Distance function and test}
 #|include: false
 #|echo: false
 #|output: false
 #|fig-show: hide

library(Distance)

dis <- readRDS(here("temp", "dis.rds"))
trunc <- 8000
conversion.factor <- convert_units("Metre", NULL, "square metre")

# Overall detection function (pooled)
d.hn <- ds(data=dis %>% filter(!is.na(dis$size)), key="hn", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=trunc)
d.hr <- ds(data=dis %>% filter(!is.na(dis$size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=trunc)


# Significant is good so it does not fit the data
gof_hn <- gof_ds(d.hn, plot = FALSE)

# P value is not significant, good
gof_hr <- gof_ds(d.hr, plot = FALSE)

# height as a covariate
d.ht <- ds(data=dis %>% filter(!is.na(dis$size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=trunc, formula = ~height)
# Region as covariate
d.re <- ds(data=dis %>% filter(!is.na(dis$size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=trunc, formula = ~Region.Label)
#Region and height
d.rh <- ds(data=dis %>% filter(!is.na(dis$size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=trunc, formula = ~height, ~Region.Label)


# Write summary table
model_tab <- summarize_ds_models(d.hn, d.hr, d.ht, d.re, d.rh, output = "plain")

# Summary of the best model and abundance and density estimates
sum_ht <- summary(d.ht)


```

```{r, Clean ds data}
 #|include: false

cluster_clean <- sum_ht$dht$clusters$D %>% 
  transmute(Label,
            "Density (km⁻²)"   = Estimate * 1e6,
            "SE (km⁻²)" = se * 1e6,
            "LCL (km⁻²)" = lcl * 1e6,
            "UCL (km⁻²)" = ucl * 1e6,
            CV = cv)

indiv_clean <- d.ht$dht$individuals$D %>% 
  transmute(Label,
            "Density (km⁻²)"   = Estimate * 1e6,
            "SE (km⁻²)" = se * 1e6,
            "LCL (km⁻²)" = lcl * 1e6,
            "UCL (km⁻²)" = ucl * 1e6,
            CV = cv)

```



This study applied point transect distance sampling technique to small cetaceans, utilising a modified version of the point transect protocol primarily employed for birds or larger cetaceans [@bucklandIntroductionDistanceSampling2001a]. The protocol was adapted to incorporate land-based surveys, enabling a systematic assessment of the cetacean distribution in the `#gls("bgtw")`{=typst}.

## Survey Results
Overall, `r  full_sum$sightings` cetacean sightings were recorded between `r format(min(full$date.x), "%d %b %Y")` and `r format(max(full$date.x), "%d %b %Y")`, point transect sampling was conducted including pilot studies and both vessel- and land-based surveys (@fig-totalmap). A total of two vessel-based surveys were conducted, accounting for `r spell_nr(full_source$boat)` of these sightings: `r spell_nr(boat_species$common_dolphin)` sightings of common dolphins (*Delphinus delphis*) and `r spell_nr(boat_species$striped_dolphin)` sightings of striped dolphins (*Stenella coeruleoalba*) and `r spell_nr(boat_species$delphinidae)` sighting where the species could not be identified. The group size for vessel-based sightings ranged from `r spell_nr(min(boat$group_size, na.rm = TRUE))` to `r spell_nr(max(boat$group_size, na.rm = TRUE))` (mean = `r round(mean(boat$group_size, na.rm = TRUE), 2)`, median = `r round(median(boat$group_size, na.rm = TRUE), 2)`, SD = ±`r round(sd(boat$group_size, na.rm = TRUE), 2)`).

![Geographic location of cetacean sightings recorded from boat-based surveys (in squares) and land-based surveys (in circles) during this project period. Where n represents the number of sightings recorded using each method.](figures/method_map.png){#fig-totalmap}

In total `r total_land$surveys` land-based surveys were conducted, resulting in `r round(sum(er$hours), 2)` total on-effort hours and 58 off-effort hours over `r length(unique(effort$date))` survey days. `r length(data$survey_id)` cetacean sightings were recorded in this sampling period, of which `r sum(data$on_effort)` were on-effort and `r spell_nr(sum(!data$on_effort))` off-effort. Of these, `r by_familiy$odontoceti` sightings were Odontoceti, including `r by_species$common_dolphin` sightings of common dolphins, `r by_species$delphinidae` sightings of unidentified dolphins where the species could not certainly be defined and `r spell_nr(by_familiy$mysteciti)` Mysticeti sightings of  Fin whales (*Balaenoptera physalus*). `r spell_nr(by_species$unidentified)` sightings the species could not be reliably identified and were recorded as unidentified (@fig-sight_map).

![All sightings collected from land-based surveys between `r format(min(full$date.x), "%d.%m.%Y")` and `r format(max(full$date.x), "%d.%m.%Y")`. The sightings were recorded from three different spots, Europa Point, Sandy Bay and Prince George's Battery (indicated as triangles).](figures/sightings_legend.png){#fig-sight_map}

The group size for land-based sightings ranged from `r min(data$group_size, na.rm = TRUE)` to `r max(data$group_size, na.rm = TRUE)` (mean = `r round(mean(data$group_size, na.rm = TRUE), 2)`, median = `r round(median(data$group_size, na.rm = TRUE), 2)`, SD = ±`r round(sd(data$group_size, na.rm = TRUE), 2)`). The overview table (@tbl-overview) showed different maximum and minimum distances as well as varying group sizes across the spots, with the farthest sighting being `r round(max(er$max_dis)/1000,2)` km away from the observation platform. The number of sightings differed significantly among sampling locations, despite equal on-effort observation time across all locations.


The most common species recorded was the Common Dolphin (n = `r species$sightings[1]`), followed by unidentified dolphins (n = `r species$sightings[2]`). The farthest sightings were unidentified dolphins at around `r round(max(species$max_dis/1000),2)` km (@tbl-overview).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-overview
#| tbl-cap: "Statistics per observation platforms over the sampling period. Pilot studies were excluded in this table. Distance is calculated from the respective observation platform."

overview_tbl
```


The number of sightings was also plotted against the distance they were recorded in a histogram (@fig-dis_spot). With a clear trend of sightings at Europa being closer to the observer compared to the other spots.

![Number of sightings against the Distance (in m) where they were recorded relative to the observation spot. Split up by the observation spots (SandyBay, Europa and PrinceGeorge (binwidth = 550)).](figures/distance_facet.png){#fig-dis_spot}

Most of the sightings were recorded at `r per_spot$spot_name[which.max(per_spot$sightings)]`, covering mainly the southern side (n = `r max(per_spot$sightings)`). And between the hours XX and XX (@fig-day_SPUE A). `#gls("spue")`{=typst} were highest at Europa (@fig-day_SPUE B, @tbl-spue_report). A Kruskal-Wallis test showed a significant difference in `#gls("spue")`{=typst} among spots (χ² = `r round(as.numeric(SPUE_kru$statistic),2)`, df = `r as.numeric(SPUE_kru$parameter)`, p = `r round(SPUE_kru$p.value,3)`), Dunn’s test, with a Benjamini-Hochberg correction, showed a significant difference between Europa and Prince George (adjusted p = `r round(SPUE_dunn$res[1,4],3)`) and between Europa and Sandy Bay (adjusted p = `r round(SPUE_dunn$res[2,4],3)`).

![Sightings over time of day (A) showed tow peaks before and after noon. Sightings per Unit Effort (SPUE) showed a significant difference between Europa and Prince George using Kruksal-Wallis and Dunn's test(B).](figures/day_SPUE_comb.png){#fig-day_SPUE}


```{r}
#| label: tbl-spue_report
#| tbl-cap: "Sightings per Unit effort were different over the three spots as well as number of individuals."



tbl_dat <- spue_report %>%
  filter(spot_name %in% active_spots$spot_name | spot_name == "TOTAL") %>%
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%                       # replaces cols_hide(geometry); use select(-geometry) if not an sf object
  select(spot_name, scans, effort_hours, sightings,
         individuals, spue_per_scan, sightings_per_h, indiv_per_h) %>%
  rename(
    "Spot Names"      = spot_name,
    "Scans"           = scans,
    "Hours"           = effort_hours,
    "Sightings"       = sightings,
    "Individuals"     = individuals,
    "SPUE (per scan)" = spue_per_scan,
    "Sightings per h" = sightings_per_h,
    "Indiv. per h"    = indiv_per_h
  )

total_row <- which(tbl_dat[["Spot Names"]] == "TOTAL")   # robust to row order

tt(tbl_dat,
   width = c(1.6, 1, 1, 1,1, 1.5, 1, 1)) |>                                        # width = 1 → full text width
  format_tt(j = c(3, 6, 7, 8), digits = 2,
            num_fmt = "decimal", num_zero = TRUE) |>             # 2 decimals incl. trailing zeros
  style_tt(i = 0, bold = TRUE) |>                                # i = 0 targets the column-label (header) row
  style_tt(i = total_row, bold = TRUE) %>% 
  style_tt(j = 2:8, align = "c")
  
  

```

## Detection model fitting
Using the `Distance` package [@millerDistanceSampling2019] a detection function was fitted per spot. Using the `#gls("hr")`{=typst} key function resulted in the lowest AIC value (hn = `r round(AIC(d.hn)[1,2],2)`; hr = `r round(AIC(d.hr)[1,2],2)`) and was therefore used for further analyis. The goodness of fit test confirmed the choice of the key function (hn p-value: `r round(gof_hn$dsgof$CvM$p,3)`; hr p-value = `r round(gof_hr$dsgof$CvM$p,3)`).
To increase the fit of the function height and region were used as covariates. Using height as covariate and in combination with the region resulted in the lowest AIC (@tbl-model_summary).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-model_summary
#| tbl-cap: "Summary of the models used to fit the detection function. The formula represents the covariates used."

tt(model_tab) %>% 
  style_tt(i = 0, bold = TRUE) %>% 
  style_tt(j = 4:7, align = "c")

```

Using the `#gls("hr")`{=typst} key function along with height as a covariate the detection function was plotted (@fig-ht_model).

```{r}
#| label: fig-ht_model
#| fig-cap: "Detection function using the hr key function along with height as covariate."

plot(d.ht, pdf = TRUE)
```


Using the best fitted detection functions the relative abundance of dolphins was calculated. The highest cluster density was estimated to be `r round(sum_ht$dht$clusters$D$Estimate[1]* 1e6, 3)` per km² in the southern sector (surveyd from Europa Point) (95 % CI: `r round(cluster_clean[1,3], 2)` - `r round(cluster_clean[1,4], 2)`; CV: `r round(cluster_clean$CV[1], 2)`)(@tbl-cluster).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-cluster
#| tbl-cap: "Group density estimates per spot using the detection function with height as covariate."

tt(cluster_clean, digits = 2) %>% 
  style_tt(i = c(0, 4), bold = TRUE)
```

The highest individual density was estimated at Europa `r round(sum_ht$dht$individuals$D$Estimate[1]* 1e6, 3)` per km² (95 % CI: `r round(indiv_clean[1,3], 2)` - `r round(indiv_clean[1,4], 2)`; CV: `r round(indiv_clean$CV[1], 2)`)(@tbl-indiv).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-indiv
#| tbl-cap: "Individual density estimates per spot using the detection function with height as covariate."

tt(indiv_clean, digits = 2) %>% 
  style_tt(i = c(0, 4), bold = TRUE)
```


<!-- Here comes a table of the abundance estimates from the ds function -->

## Kernel Density Estimation
The Kernel Density Estimation using group size as a weight variable showed the highest density around the south western tip of the peninsula (lat: XX, lon: XX). With the core area covering most of the southern sector (@fig-kde_map). The home area stretched around the whole peninsula covering most of the BGTW and every sector.


![The Kernel Density Estimation (KDE) of the sightings collected from land-based surveys between `r format(min(full$date.x), "%d.%m.%Y")` and `r format(max(full$date.x), "%d.%m.%Y")`. The KDE was calculated using group size as a weight variable. The highest density was found around the south western tip of the peninsula (lat: XX, lon: XX).). Color of grid represents the density Utilisazion distribution.](figures/density_map.png){#fig-kde_map}
